title: Distance Formula
aria_label: d equals the square root of, open parenthesis, x-sub-2 minus x-sub-1, close parenthesis squared, plus, open parenthesis, y-sub-2 minus y-sub-1, close parenthesis squared
notes: The distance formula is derived directly from the Pythagorean theorem.
It gives the straight-line (Euclidean) distance between any two points.
Order of subtraction doesn't matter — the result is squared, so negatives cancel.
If the two points share an x-value (vertical) or y-value (horizontal), the formula still works but simplifies.
applications: Verifying the classification of triangles (equilateral, isosceles, scalene).
Finding the radius of a circle given its center and a point on it.
GPS and mapping: calculating straight-line distance between coordinates.

es.title: Fórmula de la Distancia
notes: La fórmula de la distancia se deriva directamente del teorema de Pitágoras.
Proporciona la distancia en línea recta entre dos puntos.
El orden de resta no importa — el resultado se eleva al cuadrado, así que los negativos se cancelan.
La respuesta siempre es no negativa.
Si los puntos comparten un valor x o y, la fórmula funciona pero se simplifica.

ht.title: Fòmil Distans
notes: Fòmil distans lan soti dirèkteman nan teyorèm Pitagò.
Li bay distans liy dwat ant de pwen.
Lòd soustraksyon pa enpòtan — rezilta a mete nan kare, konsa negatif yo anile.
Si de pwen yo pataje valè x oswa y, fòmil la fonksyone men simplifye.
